name: lab-delta-indicator
title: Lab Delta Indicator
description: The change since the prior result, with the interval and the prior value in reach — and no verdict unless the caller supplies a significance threshold.

props.name: interval
props.description: When the prior result was taken. A delta without an interval is unreadable.

props.name: significantChange
props.description: The assay's reference change value, or the caller's threshold. Without it the badge reports the change and asserts nothing.

props.name: concerningDirection
props.description: Which direction is clinically worse, if the caller wants it marked.

clinicalNotes: A delta is only meaningful against the assay's own variability — a creatinine that moves 3 µmol/L has probably not moved at all, and a UI that renders every difference as a red arrow trains clinicians to ignore the red arrows. So without `significantChange` this is a neutral badge: an arrow is a direction, not a verdict, and the tooltip says as much. Supply the threshold and it marks the change significant, in the word as well as the colour, so a greyscale reading carries the same meaning. `concerningDirection` is what turns a significant change into a warning — the component never guesses which way is worse, because for haemoglobin it is down and for creatinine it is up.
notes: Composes badge + tooltip. Used inside lab-result-chart and lab-panel-table.
